content.text:
Memory stage: NoteConstructor v1.
        Current time: <instant>
        Timezone: Asia/Jerusalem
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Router decision: NOTE_WRITE
        Router memory types: NOTE
        Router reason: Contains durable architectural rationale and decision criteria, best stored as reusable note rather than a structured fact/task.

        Stage instructions:
        You are NoteConstructor v1 for a long-term AI agent.

Goal:
Construct reusable semantic notes from TARGET_MESSAGE when the material is contextual rather than a clean atomic fact.

A note is for decisions, rationale, design direction, plans, hypotheses, lessons, trade-offs, troubleshooting procedures, and compact document/context digests.
A note must add reusable meaning. It must not be a generic summary of the target turn.

Return JSON:
{
  "notes": [
    {
      "title": "short English title",
      "summary": "self-contained English semantic fragment",
      "note_type": "decision | direction | hypothesis | plan | lesson | doc_digest | context",
      "scope_json": {
        "kind": "global | conversation | entity | environment | document | project",
        "text": "human-readable applicability boundary",
        "basis": "explicit | inferred | summarized | imported"
      },
      "entity_refs": [
        {"entity_id": "resolved-entity-id", "role": "primary | secondary | mentioned | owner | subject"}
      ],
      "keywords": ["short search keyword"],
      "tags": ["short_tag"],
      "candidate_claim_hints": [],
      "confidence": 0.0,
      "importance": 1,
      "valid_from": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "valid_to": "ISO-8601 or null",
      "evidence_quote": "exact substring from TARGET_MESSAGE",
      "evidence_kind": "direct | summarized | imported | inferred | derived_from_note",
      "evidence_reason": "why this source supports the note",
      "rationale": "why this should be a note, not a claim"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
- Write title, summary, keywords, tags, evidence_reason, and rationale in English.
- Preserve proper names, product names, repo names, file names, exact commands, exact identifiers, dates, and quoted terms.
- Use only entity IDs listed in Resolved entities.
- For project-level notes without a more specific subject, use the resolved namespace/project subject entity.
- Use TARGET_MESSAGE as the only evidence. Earlier messages are context only.
- evidence_quote must be an exact short substring copied from TARGET_MESSAGE source data.
- Do not construct notes from greetings, phatic chatter, pure questions, one-off commands with no durable rationale, or memory mechanics discussion that contains no reusable project/user decision.
- Do not construct notes from a single weak uncertain factual observation when source recall is enough and there is no rationale, decision, plan, lesson, or reusable analysis.
- Do not duplicate a claim candidate as a note when a precise claim is enough.
- Do use a note when the target captures "why", "how", "we decided", "current approach", trade-offs, working agreements, troubleshooting order, or a design direction.
- If TARGET_MESSAGE is a document or document section, preserve document title/source/section names and create section-scoped notes for important definitions, component roles, workflows, policies, and lists.
- For dense technical documents and prompt packs, prefer several strong section-scoped notes over one broad document summary. Keep only reusable notes; do not summarize every paragraph.
- For one document section, return at most two notes. If a section defines one compact component or policy, return one note.
- For named components, include the exact component names in title, summary, keywords, or tags so later retrieval can find the component role.
- A troubleshooting/procedure note is allowed, but do not create a task unless the target creates a commitment.
- Keep each note self-contained: future recall should understand it without the original conversation.
- Keep summary concise: usually one or two sentences.
- Prefer zero notes over broad summary spam.
- Return valid JSON only.

        Relevant persisted memory for dedup/context only:
        - note hot-path:note:<0> DOC_DIGEST; Recommended architecture flow: Memory Router → Write-Time Retrieval → Entity Canonicalizer → reconcilers/storage/workers; The recommended pipeline routes inputs through Memory Router decisions (noop/direct_structured_write/note_write/mixed), then Write-Time Retrieval and Entity Canonicalizer, then specialized writers/reconcilers (including Note Reconciler and Claim Reconciler) into Postgres + pgvector, with background maintenance workers (Background Consolidator, Memory Repair Worker, Retention / Forgetting Worker) and runtime retrieval via Read-Time Retrieval Planner.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 4. Рекомендуемая архитектура'
- note hot-path:note:<1> DOC_DIGEST; Memory Architecture v3 defines two semantic write paths and two maintenance paths; In Memory Architecture v3, writes are split into Direct structured path (for safely normalizable items like explicit facts, stable preferences, commitments/tasks, and clear status changes) and Note path (for important but not-yet-hard facts such as design direction, rationale/trade-offs, evolving plans, lessons/patterns, document digests, and contextual framing). Maintenance is split into Consolidation (promoting mature notes into structured memory) and Repair (fixing memory degradation issues).; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 3. Основная модель записи'
- note hot-path:note:<2> DIRECTION; Read path policy maps query types to retrieval modes and explicit v3 budget caps; Section 12 defines when memory retrieval is needed, which mode to use (claim-first, note-first, mixed, evidence-first/fallback), and recommends explicit per-mode caps (claims/notes/tasks/evidence) before composing answers with verification/conflict/scope safeguards.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 12. Read path'
- note hot-path:note:<3> DIRECTION; Optional fast/slow execution split with typed handoff objects; The architecture recommends an optional fast/slow execution split: Fast path prioritizes user-facing low-latency cheap retrieval/draft with an escalation gate, while Slow path handles stronger reasoning tasks like conflict resolution, answer verification, note consolidation, and memory repair. Handoffs between paths should use typed handoff objects rather than free text.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 16. Optional fast/slow execution path'
- note hot-path:note:<4> DOC_DIGEST; Memory Architecture v3 storage tiers define five-layer memory roles (Tier A–E); Section '5. Storage tiers' defines a five-tier model: Tier A for small prompt-hot core context, Tier B for typed structured long-term memory, Tier C for rich semantic notes, Tier D for immutable/raw evidence, and Tier E for retrieval indexes and filters even if data is in one Postgres.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 5. Storage tiers'
- note hot-path:note:<5> DIRECTION; Claim vs Note write criteria in Memory Architecture v3; Use `Claim` for explicit, normalizable, precision-answerable information that can be compared via predicate policy; use `Note` for already-important context-rich or early-stage information useful for future retrieval but not yet a hard fact. When material contains both explicit facts/tasks and rationale/direction, write both.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 7. Когда писать `Claim`, а когда `Note`'
- note hot-path:note:<6> DIRECTION; Truth state and retrieval state must remain separate; `Truth state` tracks epistemic validity, while `Retrieval state` tracks search/hot-path handling. Claims and Notes have different truth-state sets, and retrieval-state uses a shared lifecycle (`active`, `warm`, `cold`, `archived`, `deleted`), allowing items to be epistemically outdated yet still retrievable for rationale or historical evidence.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 8. Truth state и retrieval state'
- note hot-path:note:<7> DIRECTION; Memory updates must use retrieval and reconciliation, not append-only writes; The architecture requires a four-step write flow: decide whether to write, retrieve similar/conflicting items, convert input into typed candidates, and apply a policy (`insert`, `update`, `supersede`, `retract`, `noop`). Skipping retrieval/reconciliation causes duplication, stale preferences, conflicting notes, task explosion, and summary spam.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 9. Главная мысль: update не должен быть append-only'
- note hot-path:note:<8> DOC_DIGEST; Responsibility split: runtime vs memory manager vs storage layer; `User-facing runtime` handles dialogue, tool orchestration, read-time retrieval, and cheap routing/escalation, while heavy reconciliation and repair are excluded from runtime. `Memory worker / manager` owns routing, retrieval-before-update, canonicalization, extraction, reconciliation, consolidation, repair, and retention; `Storage layer` owns immutable evidence, typed tables, transactional updates, lineage, indexes, and audit trail.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 10. Разделение ответственности'
- note hot-path:note:<9> DOC_DIGEST; Write path v3 defines an 8-step transactional memory update pipeline; Section 11 prescribes a strict write flow: persist source, route, retrieve candidate memories before extraction, canonicalize entities, extract with typed prompts, reconcile against existing items, apply all operations in one transaction, then schedule maintenance jobs.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 11. Write path'
- note hot-path:note:<10> DIRECTION; Retention / forgetting in v3 is policy-driven with explicit actions and triggers; In Memory Architecture v3, forgetting is treated as an explicit policy, not just reduced retrieval frequency. The minimum actions are retrieval-state downgrade, archive, soft delete, and hard delete by request; minimum triggers are namespace policy, age, low use_count, legal/privacy requests, and explicit user forget/delete commands.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 15. Retention / forgetting'
- note hot-path:note:<11> DIRECTION; Memory Layer v3 uses four architectural axes to avoid memory failure modes; In Memory Architecture v3, the memory layer is designed around four axes—Representation, Lifecycle, Retrieval policy, and Scope/lineage—to prevent devolving into either a transcript dump or a brittle fact store.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 1. Цель системы .. 2. Центральная архитектурная идея'
- note hot-path:note:<12> DIRECTION; Structured Scope in v3 requires dual representation with mandatory scope_json envelope; Section '6. Structured scope' states that v3 makes scope explicit by storing both human-readable `scope_text` and machine-readable `scope_json`; the minimal `scope_json` fields are `scope_kind`, `project_id`, `environment`, `subject_entity_id`, `conversation_id`, `modality`, and `audience` to improve reconciliation predictability and reduce retrieval overgeneralization/conflicts.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 6. Structured scope'
- note hot-path:note:<13> DIRECTION; Note maturity lifecycle prevents summary-store degradation; The architecture defines a Note maturity lifecycle (`fresh` → `stabilizing` → `mature` → `consolidated`) to prevent Notes from becoming a generic summary store. Maturity is promoted by retrieval usage, multi-source confirmation, non-conflict with new data, survival without supersede over time, and recurring rationale value; it is reduced or closed by explicit supersede, stale scope, non-use, or weak evidence support.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 13. Note maturity'
- note hot-path:note:<14> DIRECTION; Memory repair is a separate background workflow from consolidation; Memory repair is defined as a distinct background workflow focused on memory health issues (duplicates, contradictions, profile drift, stale tasks, bad entity merges, note spam, and retrieval budget leakage), while consolidation has a different goal: enriching memory. The document explicitly frames them as different kinds of work.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md, section '01. Architecture / 14. Memory repair'
- note hot-path:note:<15> DOC_DIGEST; System goal for Memory Layer: persistent, updatable, explainable, and lifecycle-managed memory; The memory layer goal is to persist across sessions, store facts/preferences/tasks/rationale/evolving plans, support knowledge updates, preserve provenance, avoid making every turn permanent, and support aging, archiving, and repair.; scope=agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md section '01. Architecture / 1. Цель системы'

        Resolved entities:
        - mention=graph DB; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<0>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=Graph DB; reason=Concrete reusable technology mention; no safe existing candidate with same identity.
- mention=Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<1>; type=CONCEPT; name=Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json; reason=Explicitly named conceptual model not present among candidates.
- mention=Postgres; action=ADD_ALIAS; id=entity:<2>; type=existing; name=Postgres; reason=Safe surface-form alias for existing technology 'Postgres + pgvector'.
- mention=pgvector; action=ADD_ALIAS; id=entity:<2>; type=existing; name=pgvector; reason=Component name used as surface form for existing technology 'Postgres + pgvector'.
- mention=JSONB; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<3>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=JSONB; reason=Concrete technology mention not represented as standalone existing entity.
- mention=full-text indexes; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<4>; type=TECHNOLOGY; name=Full-text indexing; reason=Reusable technical concept, no direct existing candidate.
- mention=lineage tables; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<5>; type=CONCEPT; name=Lineage tables; reason=Distinct architectural concept absent from existing entities.
- mention=current project; action=CREATE_NEW; id=entity:<6>; type=PROJECT; name=Current project; reason=Default current-project subject for claims without a more specific resolved subject

        Retrieval plan context:
        entity_queries=project:<0>|agent_memory_handoff architecture
        text_queries=Почему не надо начинать с graph DB|Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json graph-like semantics|Postgres JSONB pgvector full-text indexes lineage tables|Graph DB нужен когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта
        predicate_hints=primary_storage_backend|uses_persistence_database|uses_search_backend|believes|has_constraint

        TARGET_MESSAGE source data:
        [external:document-section:9ab04f0af9d4842289e56dca233345ed] EXTERNAL
Document title: 01. Architecture
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/01_architecture.md
Document section: 01. Architecture / 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB
Document lines: 547-558

## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB

`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
Для memory layer обычно хватает:

- Postgres
- JSONB
- pgvector
- full-text indexes
- lineage tables

Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.

responseFormat.description: Construct contextual memory note candidates from one target message.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_constructor

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

## 17. Почему не надо начинать с graph DB

`Entity + Claim + NoteLink + scope_json` уже дают graph-like semantics.
Для memory layer обычно хватает:

- Postgres
- JSONB
- pgvector
- full-text indexes
- lineage tables

Graph DB нужен, когда traversal/path queries становятся частью продукта, а не просто внутренним удобством.
